reviews.review_text: Path of Exile 2 delivers deep character customization, challenging combat, and a much stronger campaign experience than most action RPGs. The visuals, boss fights, and skill system are impressive and rewarding for players who enjoy experimenting with builds. However, the game can feel overwhelming for newcomers, and some progression and balance issues are still noticeable during early access. Overall, it’s a very promising ARPG with excellent gameplay depth, but it may require patience and a willingness to learn its complex systems.

I don't recommend this game purely because of the support that is GGG. Bought the game a few months ago, but didn't really enjoy it at the time and refunded it. I only played for 48 minutes at that time. Bought it again at 50% off this weekend, tried to log in and my account was locked. 

Contacted GGG support, they said it's locked because of an unauthorized refund and apparently this counts as stealing from them. I contact steam support, they confirm that the refund was legitimate and since the ban was a outside of steam and through GGG's system, they can't do much. 

I contacted GGG support yet again and am met with silence. 

If refunds through steam are counted as "unauthorized refunds" then perhaps you shouldn't have steam be able to issue refunds on your behalf. 

If people are exploiting premium currency by buying the game and then refunding, maybe YOU should have some exploit fix in place and not punish innocent/unknowing players for this.

I am glad Steam has decent refund policies for instances like this.

Installed the game: “Wow, this looks incredible.”

Reached first boss: “Okay, cool mechanics.”

Died 14 times: “Fair enough.”

Opened the passive tree: “What in the doctoral thesis is this?”

Found a new weapon: +5% stun duration against enemies that are on fire during a lunar eclipse

My current build:

* emotionally compromised
* rolling constantly
* surviving on vibes
* somehow scaling poison, lightning, and regret simultaneously

Meanwhile the game: “You have insufficient spirit.”

Brother I have insufficient will to live.

Loot system:

* kill ancient horror from beyond reality
* receive: sandals

Every boss fight feels personal.
Every respec costs the GDP of a small nation.
Every veteran player explains mechanics like they’re teaching quantum physics.

And yet … After 6 straight hours of suffering:

* boss finally dies
* health bar at 1 pixel
* hands shaking
* garbage loot drops

10/10.
See you tomorrow.
This was my first time with any poe game, and I was not disappointed.  This is by far the best arpg ive played, and im a huge fan boy now.

So much time and effort for such little reward it transcends frustration and spirals into brain melting hysteria. 
The look of this game is likely it's only redeeming quality because its honestly great, they really managed to encompass the graphical fidelity that we always expected of Blizzard and it handles as well too. However where Diablo 4 felt too fast paced in order to get you to their end game grind, PoE 2 is the complete and total opposite and in the worst way. This game is SUCH A SLOG Act 2 was mentally AND physically draining and Act 3 has made me quit...twice (I've come back to PoE 2 - 4 times). You find yourself grinding for hours, going through mazes of these enormous sprawling maps that feel like they never end and the Vaal temples that take 45 minutes to an hour to complete and provide practically nothing, I just started skipping them by end of Act 3 I just didnt care anymore because I wasnt getting gear that was worth putting in the Vaal temple room upgrades. Also if you filled up most or all of the Vaal board layout it just goes on and on and on. You're just grinding for hours every day for orbs just to maybe upgrade and replace 1 piece of gear that maybe had 1 good modifier that you have to trade off for another good modifier. None of the gear ever feels well crafted enough for your character, oh look a melee skill modifier for my Sorc, or int mod for Warr.
The skills...yikes, I never hated the skills in PoE 1. I actually thought PoE 1 was a fun spin on the typical D4 playstyle I was used to at the time, but the skills in PoE 2 feel like an after thought that was rushed together. The skills really need a desperate overhaul, I used the same 2 sorc skills and 3 witch skills for practically 60% of the game. My favorite class to play is usually a minion build and Witches Minions + Lich build is closest, but it does NOT have the same feel. 
This game is so mentally draining and gut wrenchingly unrewarding that I can't stand it anymore. I really want to play a good arpg dungeon looter, and today PoE 2 almost made me want to go back and play D4. 
I'm out, I'll watch the new update on a stream but I can't see myself coming back in a couple weeks unless it really gets an overhaul on literally everything.

I usually feel like I'm too old for this game and completely lack the time or patience for it. Yet, I still end up jumping in every second season, and once I start, I can't put it down until I reach the endgame.

For me, the real fun is theorycrafting, testing out new builds, and farming items from absolute scratch. I don't really care about the crazy min-maxing in the deep endgame right now. This is by far the best game in the genre. The animations are clean and it is incredibly satisfying when a build finally comes together and the entire screen just explodes.

I've played every league since early access launched, and at this point the game has become genuinely exhausting. 

The core issue is the campaign and early game. Replaying the same story from scratch every few months is simply not fun, and the early game does nothing to make that easier. Your character feels sluggish, enemies are overtuned for how little your skills do, and the whole thing drags in ways that feel more like friction than challenge. By the time you finally escape into the endgame, you've already resented the journey.

Not recommended, unless GGG fundamentally reconsiders the campaign-replay loop.

It is a good game if you want to spend thousands of hours.

Pace is slow and quality of life is really ♥♥♥♥.

Basic things still missing like Auto Organize inventory, quest marks, auto equip etc 

Game has potential, but needs a lot of improvements, by new you will suffer if you don't try hard on it.

EDIT: MY ACCOUNT HAS BEEN LOCKED AFTER I COMMENTED HERE, I HAVE NO IDEA WHY.

No answer from the support.

Runaway from it.

Simply the greatest ARPG of all time, and it's not even 1.0. The developers (Grinding Gear Games) are extremely communicative with the community, it's almost unmatched; and they certainly know what they are doing. 

The game is absolutely beautiful and feels amazing. Controlling your character and the animations are super fluid. This game ruined most other games for me. Proceed with caution!

I thought topdown games looked boring

Then I downloaded PoE 2 "just to try it"

That was several days ago.

Now my sleep schedule is gone, my browser has 47 tabs open for build guides, and I still have absolutely no idea what half the crafting materials do.

10/10 psychological warfare

Can we at least acknowledge that this game pretending they're doing a favor by having a free weekend on a game that was advertised as "going to be free" is weird behavior? It's been over a year. This isn't a beta test. They're releasing "major expansions" This is a cashgrab from a greedy company.
I absolutely hate the campaign. Especially act 1-3. Feels very slow and isn't fun.

I love POE, been playing it for +5 years, but the POE 2 campaign completely broke me, it's just extremely boring, very slow charecter progression, I just hit act 2 and it just felt like a chore not like I was having fun.
Let's be honest, nobody cares about the campaign once they beat it for the first time, and after they beat it for the second time it just becomes a chore, a +12 hour chore just to get to the actual game.
At least in POE 1 the campaign is very short compared to POE 2, and you have movement skills and flasks so it doesn't feel like you're walking through molasses the entire game.
Honestly I'd rather just pay the full game price every season just to be able to skip the campaign.

I like the game, I like the way the game feels and looks, but the game doesn't like someone like me. It feels like unless you want to look at how someone else plays the game it'll make you feel like you don't know what your doing. You feel like you are punished if you try to experiment with the crafting system, considering the resources you need are not just thrown at you. I feel no matter what I try every season the game just wants me to feel dumb and have to look at how the "pros" play, when I just want to have fun and try my own thing. If you like the feeling of your parents telling you that you'll never be anything in life no matter how hard you try unless you do what your better, older, and wiser brother does, then this game is a 9/10. if you want something to get on a couple hours a day, grind some loot and get stronger, this ain't it. One mistake costs hours depending on what point of the game you are at and how well you managed all your resources. People will see this as a me problem, which is fine, but I've tried. I just don't feel wanted here and my time is not respected.

Its not a fun game, people say this game is great because it has so much build variety, but its a complete lie, when you offer too many choices you just overwhelm players and push them into guides, the talent tree is a mess and you cant make a decent build unless you know a big chunk of the whole tree.

When you pick a class like in any rpg, you want to play as that class right? Well not here, most guides tell you to use abilities and weapons from another class, so even if you pick monk, the guide tells you to play as a caster or a ranger, this makes no sense, and if you ignore any guide, eventually you will hit a wall because the game is overly complex for no reason, so when you do hit the wall your next option is stop playing the game and get PHD on the talent tree and on the dozens of weird systems the game has for small upgrades or... follow a guide and stomp everything.

I was not a fan of PoE1 and was hoping PoE2 would be different, but exactly the same game, they should have called this an expansion, the only meaningful change i can see is now we have a dodge roll.

In the great words of Terry A. Devis "An idiot admires complexity, smart people admire simplicity" this is one of those games that is complex for the sake of seeming complex.

Overall if you like diablo like games i would be skeptical of PoE2, its a shame Diablo has good class fantasy but reduces the difficulty to a mobile game, and then PoE2 does not understand class fantasy and you need to study 120 options to make the correct one, or blindly follow a guide, while the difficulty and combat is good.
All problems from the first game, no solutions. Nerfing random builds mid league bricking fun for others.

0.5 is the worst league ever. The devs spent 6 months working on it. And what do we get in the end?

The game crashes every time I teleport to town. After that, I can't launch it for 10–30 minutes (because when I log in, the game starts stuttering, graphics quality drops, and it crashes again with no error).

Many people have complained about the Deadlock error.

Loot filter sounds only started working on the third day.

Build planners don't work properly.

Invincible mobs in Abyss.

I honestly don't understand what they spent HALF A YEAR doing for the biggest update launch. I'm scared to imagine what will happen on the full release.

Been here since early access was released. I do NOT UNDERSTAND how the early game and campaign is still this abhorent. I cannot stand the barrier to the endgame anymore.

Stun mechanic in the start of the game is just plain horrible. Getting 2 shot by ranged mobs hiding around a corner is also kinda ♥♥♥♥. The map layouts during the campaign are still ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ huge and looking for a cave entrance or exit for 10-15 minutes every quest is just not fun.

first of all, games dont need to be 140+ gigs, its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ridiculous, secondly games shouldnt need to be installed after installing them over 9 hours, this is the only game I own that does this, this last update did something that i cant even understand, been doing everything under the sun to boost the download speed but it's literally only this game, been downloading for like 18 hours now and its halfway done its 2nd full install for some reason. Optimize this ♥♥♥♥ already ffs.Finally got in and the shaders they spent 2 days installing were corrupted lol, just gear screen everytime i enter a new zone. the game is good but this is making me want to just cancel the install and say ♥♥♥♥ it til you guys smarten up. Helldivers 2 could remove usless ♥♥♥♥ to lower their game size and diable has the option to install the hd graphics and ♥♥♥♥ if u want essentially cutting the size in half. Get with the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ program  grinding gear.

Every season they force you to play through the campaign before you can unlock endgame content. You clear map after map hoping for a meaningful upgrade which you never get. I went from act 2 to endgame without any upgrades on multiple slots. If you are this obsessed with forcing people to play the campaign then at least put some guaranteed upgrades so it doesn't become such a slog. 

Crafting system is pure RNG for the most part and the only way to do deterministic crafting is by farming endlessly to have enough resources for it. That would require you to play this game 7/24. 

It's a game where time you invest into it is not rewarded ever.

I cannot recommend this game due to a predatory and completely backward account management backend.

A few months ago, I purchased the Path of Exile 2 Early Access pack on Steam. After playing for a bit, I went into the privacy settings and requested an account deletion, assuming it would simply wipe my player data and characters.

The trap: Grinding Gear Games permanently binds your real-money purchases to their specific account profile rather than your Steam license. Because I deleted the profile, the $30 Early Access license I paid for was completely thrown into the shredder with it. Support will completely stonewall you, stating they "have no way of restoring a deleted purchase," even if you provide a 100% legitimate Steam transaction ID and receipt.

To make it worse, you get caught in a broken cross-platform loop: Steam refuses to let you repurchase the game because it thinks you already own it, while GGG's servers refuse to let you log in because your profile has no license.

If you ever think you might want to take a break and wipe your data, do not touch this game. Their customer service policy would rather lose a paying player permanently over a basic database linking error than manually credit a verified Steam receipt. Moving on to games that actually respect their players' money.

It seems there is a clear direction missing. What does GGG want? 
Hard Bosses like Dark Souls/Elden Ring? Then make them fun and not annoying.
A fast paced classic ARPG? Then make it fast and don't let the players "explore" way to huge areas while not even implementing movement skills like blink. While forcing them to play an excruciating long campaign on each frickin single character.

Too much reliance on currency and crafting. Was fun up until it took the loot drop dopamine kick away.

May be for others, but not for me.

Game is good except one thing.....

The honor system in ascendary trials is the stupidest addition that was from a previous league.
TLDR the honor system is a second health bar but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, this is because bosses and certain attacks can take a lot of honor away in one hit like 500+ honor with resistances. 
The honor system exists literally only punishes melee, tanky and mid range build which is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ baffling to tell a chunk of the player base to essentially "get ♥♥♥♥♥♥ idiot, you should of choosen long range or magic playstyle you idiot" Seriously it is ass to play a close up melee class in these trials and lose at the boss room because you got hit three-four times and you didn't die because you ran out of hp or hp flask no because your honor ran out :L
Before you think it yes i'm well aware you can get honor from certain rooms but its only after completing a room and the amount you get is random and to top it all off personally most of the time my paths always almost everytime have a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ curse/debuff if i take the honor restore and thats not even the worse part some of these ♥♥♥♥♥♥ curses are flat out stupid and shouldn't be in the game like for example:
brought in some relics to increase your maxium honor and reduce how much you lose from getting hit? haha no ♥♥♥♥ you here's a curse those relics effects by half of their effectiveness....
or better this one i got
there a  curse that literally stops you from getting any more honor restored once you get it and i got this after choosing a room to get honor back that was before the boss room...now granted you can i think see sometimes what the curse is but cursed rewards i do believe you cannot tell what you'll get and as i said some of them are just plainly awful and too crippling especially for melee and mid range builds.

The honor system wouldn't be so bad if shops were more frequent and boons also, but they just aren't and so they can't even out the awfullness of curses and the ♥♥♥♥♥♥ honor system.

Now i do love poe2 its very fun to play but i just loath a system that ♥♥♥♥♥ on two types of builds/playstyles that is pretty much not optional because to ignore your ascendary class is a massive dmg/dps loss to pass over it, GGG needs to rework honor by simply making buffs or ways to boost your honor resistance easier instead of just from rewards that rarely appear at the end of rooms, like they could add unique enemies when killed give a resistance to your honor for killing them or optional side objectives in rooms like kill X amount of rare enemies to get rewarded with X% boost to your maxium amount of honor. just generally stuff like that so it feels more like an actual good roguelite mod rather than a tacted on roguelite mode to pad out play time accidentally because GGG didn't properly consider how one mechanic would massively punish certain play styles and builds.

This game's balance is horrendous. You see something new and cool, you make a build for it. It either does not work due to a bug, or it gets removed due to being "unbalanced" the next day.

Every single ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ time since release I have had to change how I play the game unwillingly, because the developers wish me to. And I am not even someone who follows and looks up builds.

I do not recommend this game so long as the understanding of the balance of the developers are "remove it" rather than "nerf it".

I've been playing these games since Diablo 1 when I was 20 years old.  I've seen everything under the sun come and go, live and die.  So, here's my review:

It's so strange how this game has such a cult following.  Any bad review, any mention of a game flaw (I'm sorry, but in reality, nothing is perfect... including your messianic game) is met with the quickest, non-thinking vitriol.  It's like the top 2% actually learn talking points to defend the game from all sides.

Truthfully, I quit the game because I'm someone who actually enjoys creating my own builds.  There should be hundreds of viable builds per class with this many skill variables, not 3.  Versatility that works in practice is the hallmark of quality game design.

There are those 2% (who are mostly those who copy/paste created builds by spreadsheet laden wizards) who can kill a boss in 3 hits.  The rest of humanity takes 10 to 20 minutes of white knuckle fighting.  So, those who are creative and like to play builds for fun, and not necessarily peak efficiency, have to suffer.

There are many, many people who have quit this game already.  The forums, everywhere, are full of ex-players who lamented the difficulty, increased to absurd levels to satisfy the top echelon of basement dwellers and knuckle draggers, who left disappointed and with empty hands from a lack of a lasting satisfactory game experience.

Here's a dev tip:  Since copy/paste builds are the focus of the game, simply:  

1.  (Since the fun of creating your own build, and that build be survivable was never the dev's intention) Allow verified, working builds to upload to a server and be vetted, then integrate that server into the game. Then just click a box in game to a build you want to use for that character.  99% of the game completed!

2.  Walk around and click the mouse button and the "a s d f" keys a lot until you get to a boss.  Now ROLL! 
Roll again!  And... roll!  Congratulations!  You're now in what was formerly the top 2%, and are the star of the show!  The bosses are balanced around your single button click of a build!  

Now you see how stupid the premise is for the average, targeted microtransaction (whale) gamer.

I changed to a negative vote due to a fraud like action done by GGG recently involving a sale marketed as a discount,  but they have changed the value it is worth by lowering the actualy bonus points that lead to consumer interest in purchasing. Therefore it is not discounted at all and it is advertised as a discounted early access package however it is full price for value offered according their own measuring. In my nation it breaks the law and they really need to compensate everyone who purchased this package and fix it.  The game is good but I can't support such deceptive fraud like behaviors, which are morally wrong, and if they do not compensate and fix this mess I will indefinely leave this negative.

Path of Exile 2 is easily one of the most satisfying ARPGs I've played in years. The combat feels impactful, bosses are memorable, and every class has enough depth to keep experimenting with new builds.

Unlike many modern ARPGs that focus on mindless screen-clearing, PoE 2 rewards positioning, timing, and learning boss mechanics. Every upgrade feels meaningful, and finally overcoming a difficult encounter is incredibly satisfying.

The game is still in Early Access, so there are balance issues, performance hiccups, and some systems that need more polish. But the foundation is already excellent. The visuals, atmosphere, soundtrack, and skill system show just how much passion Grinding Gear Games put into this project.

If you enjoy challenging ARPGs with deep character customization and long-term progression, this is absolutely worth playing. Even in its unfinished state, it already feels like one of the strongest competitors in the genre.

Game IS fun.
but Path of Microtransactions.
Buying Stash and Inventory Tabs and Account slots is hot garbage.

My wife and I got bamboozled by the recent pack pricing changes. Tricked us with a sale price on that new supporter pack that's not a sale at all but a terrible version of the old early access pack. Jerks.

I refunded the game moths ago when I did not feel like playing it saw it was on sale thought it again my account was locked  I contacted support they threatened to ban me for  refunding through steam and not there website for pe2 they said I would have to buy another 30 dollar bundle to give them there money that i refunded back so in response i refunded the game a second time how the support conversation went 

(me) The Path of Exile 2 account was locked. I don't know why I played for like 20 mins, refunded the game a few months later (now), re-bought the game, and now my account is locked. I don't remember my character name or anything like that. This is my Steam ID what I used to play on  76561198859091039

(me) Sep 5, 2025 -  Purchase was refund is when the refund happened i genuinely didn't know about this issue. I just saw the game was on sale and thought i would give it another try

You've used more points on your account than you've paid for, which is why your account has been locked. You'll need to repurchase the charged back amount in order to regain access to your account.

Kind regards,

(me) how would i do that

(support) Hey there,

To go ahead with this, purchase the Path of Exile 2 Early Access Supporter Pack (which contains 300 points) from the Path of Exile 2 Steam page using the Steam account connected to your Path of Exile account and let us know once you've done so.

After this is complete we will restore access to your Path of Exile account.

Kind regards,
Sian

i then refunded the game again also they said i used more points on my account  than i paid for, literally never used point do even know what they were till i looked it up

POE2 is an ARPG so heavily cribbed from Diablo that I'm amazed Activision didn't sue. It has the standard ARG formula of running around and killing everything that moves. Crucially, it has that awesome ARG feeling of walking through a zone like a hurricane, felling all that oppose you with a light boop and a laugh. If you must have that, then play this game.

Everything else about this game blows. Dozens of little useless currencies to clog up your stash; a campaign so painfully generic you wonder why they bothered to inflict it upon us at all (oh, wow, another betrayal, can't believe it...); the promise of slogging through that same campaign (10 to 15 hours at max blitz) for every new character every new season. Cosmetic microtransactions that don't even look good, instead just spewing out-of-place color vomit from various wings and effects. More microtransactions to buy more storage space so you can store all the crap currencies. Loot so spammy that the game includes an item regex filter so you don't even see 90% of what drops at endgame.

Fans of the game often point to the big skill tree. Yeah, its big. Is it good? No. Its just big. Everyone's just using a guide to tell them where to click anyways. Using a massive, bloated, wandering pile of +5% to damage nodes to compensate for a complete lack of game design is essential POE.

Balance is piss poor. The "meta" classes are 3 to 4 times stronger and faster than their lesser tier counterparts. You wanted to play a sorceress? Sorry, should have played an ice arrow deadeye and had the same ice elemental build with several multiples more damage and no remnant management!

Oh, and don't forget the bugs and performance issues. Its "early access", after all. Visual UX bugs; trials that can't be completed so you cannot gain the related ascendancy points; server rubber banding. I have a decent enough computer, but POE makes it sound like a jet engine any time water comes on screen.

I get the hunger for the ARG experience. It is so much fun to melt entire screens like a god. But this game holds that damn experience *hostage* and bleeds you so much you'd think they worked for the government.

Do not buy if your in the middle east, there is no middle east servers. The nearest server is in Singapore and its really bad you will be rubber banding around everywhere. EU servers are better but you will have 150 ping and its just lagging and stuttering. Impossible to play hardcore or the end game. This is a game where ping really matters. The whole world has their own server except the middle east, very unfair and not good
great game, poorly coded 10k of shaders everytime you enter a new map? fix your game I'm not stressing my processor because you cant code

I tried this game when it first dropped and it was cool but unfinished. I came back a over a year later to play the new content only to find out that my characters names had been taken away and recycled back into the general player pool because I was away too long and now they are demanding that I rename my characters in order to use them. This is no way to reward early adopters. CAN NOT RECOMMEND ON THESE GROUNDS

Banned me because I refunded their game, and then repurchased it, outstanding job, support team is AI, multiple messages sent, same response, you will not speak to a real person from Grinding Gear Games if you ever need support, do not waste your money on this.

11 hrs in two days. its good. really addicting. i personally enjoyed diablo 4. after playing this i can see why people didn't. its got the loop down, cool areas, fun classes.

although positive, i will note that I am also upset about the marketing for this weekend. it is not 50% off, it is $15 worth of in game coin for 15 dollars. NOT 15 dollars for 30 dollars worth. I would have boughten a new 15 dollar package, there was no reason to market it as the $30 package. so I don't know why they did that, and it feels very odd considering it's for new players. it gave my first purchase for this game a weird feeling. maybe it wasn't intended to be deceptive, but regardless it kind of was, so i hope they address it.

The game is becoming a masterpiece, this latest update, in terms of content, was one of the best and biggest GGG has ever released. However, it's also one of the worst in terms of optimization. They prioritized a colossal launch that crashes whenever you need to visit the Hub from Act 2 onwards. The biggest difficulty isn't the game itself, but playing it acceptably. I'll leave the game with a negative review until they optimize the areas with HORRIBLE performance. Overall, the game is becoming good, but it's still not what I expect in terms of experience.
I imagine that anyone playing in hardcore mode this season must be cursing ten generations of GGG's heads. I would be.

Is Path Of exile 2 a good game? yes it is - its actually brillant! 

however there are some things that i wonder about or feel is strange - for instance the current version 0.5, which said to be the last one before 1.0 release, dont have swords, axes, or daggers as weapons. To think like something as basic as these weapons are not in an ARPG that is at the end of its early acess version is.... strange to say the least. 

I hope they will bring these weapons - and some of the classes that use these weapons (like the Dualist or the Shadow) to the game before, or at the release of 1.0.
a cool armour set in this game costs more than elden ring with all DLC, servers also suck pure ass

Lag makes this frustratingly unplayable
Soooo much stuttering server LAG! (800Mbps symetric optic fiber- 55ms ping to gateway)

Pressing ESC closes the game while on the menu? What is this? a MS-DOS Game?
Can't use mouse on menus while playing with controller even thought the cursor is visible on screen.

Microtransactions are STUPIDLY EXPENSIVE, like a whole game for a set of cosmetic armor and no regionally adjusted prices for the 85% of the world population that can´t earn more than 10usd an hour.

Can´t complain too much about the poor optimization for 4gb vram gpus as it seems like a modern gaming issue but performance could be better. Still enjoyable with NVIDIA DLSS.

Gameplay is what you expect for an ARPG and it very good at what it does, when it's actually playable.

This would be a positive recommendation if it weren´t for the lag issues.

I feel like I have to voice my experience because otherwise the positive reviews from all the fanboys will make it look as if this game is an absolute godsend.

Bought this game today on a "sale", which by the way is not a sale but rather a dirty marketing trick. If you normally pay £20 or whatever for the game and get 300 MTX points then now you're paying £10 for the game and get 150 MTX points. So realistically it's just a different pack, not a sale, is it? But ok, we'll let this scummy marketing trick slide.

I install the game (which is by the way a whopping 140GB!) and set out to create my first character. Just like in a massive multiplayer game where player identities matter (right?) every single character name is taken. Why?... Why can't I have a character name I like in a single player game? This is not an MMO where player interactions are a thing. It's a single player monster slaying game.

Anyway, I find one that finally doesn't exist, get into the game and start sloooowly walking towards my first objective (imagine Diablo 4 slow times two). DISCONNECTED. I log back in, get my first weapon, kill the first few mobs, level 2 (yay) - DISCONNECTED. Log back in, I'm somehow back at the starting point with no items or progression (does this game have no concept of saving progression?) so I start everything again.

Finally get to the first "town" which is a small, ugly and dark area. Talk to NPCs and head out to the next area. Same dark and barely legible visuals. I slay all the monsters, uncover all the fog of war, find some relatively decent items that I equip and head for the next area. A few steps before I enter - DISCONNECTED. I gather all my strength not to smash my keyboard and log back in again.

I appear in the town - all my items are gone, all my progression is gone and I have to start everything from scratch. So I do. I head out into the same dark, bleak area and clear it until I find an entrance to a burrow. I head in, and find a pillar where I can get a gem. Activate it and start clearing all the spiders and maggots. Because the area is extremely tight and narrow they block me in and there is just no way to dodge or evade so they kill me. I respawn back at the entrance, run back, find a way to kill them and proceed further into the dungeon to find a boss.

New gear here I come! Boss isn't too difficult and I get it down to about 30% health before I DISCONNECT!

Needless to say this is the end of the road for me and this game. It's just not serious. Being in London and connecting to London servers on a 1.1Gbit/s line that literally never has any connection issues and not being able to play for more than 5 minutes is borderline absurd. I could forgive if this was some small indie company but GGG must be raking in millions from all the microtransactions yet they can't afford to host decent servers.

By the way the 150 points you get by buying this "discounted" version will get you exactly f**k all. 4 stash tabs is best you can get. Want more stash tabs? $60. The "GG" in "GGG" definitely doesn't stand for "good game".
poe 2 ♥♥♥♥ game. server lag. bugs and all te time black screan

I played the demo and the free weekend and then I bought the game. Including the demo and the free weekend 16 hours was counted against my playtime instantly. Those 16 hours of gameplay was great. I created a couple characters and tested them out to see which one I liked more. I replayed a few characters through Act 1 instead of advancing to Act 2 quickly. Everything was great. The game played great.

Then I advanced to Act 2 and the entire game became unplayable. Stuttering. Freezing. Lag. I tried everything that is available on google and it did not fix anything. Did not even improve... Shaders. Cache. Settings. Rendering. No amount of reduction on graphic settings did anything at all either. No improvement if you play solo either. All of a sudden the entire game became unplayable - and even if I was to create a new character and restart Act 1 - the game now stutters all the time.

The game stopped working for me entirely.

There seems to be nothing that can be done on the user end.

I do not know how to feel about this.

Kinda sad, company is so many years in business and still has network issues in production.

path of doing the same ♥♥♥♥ over and over again dodge cast dodge cast. dodge cast dodge cast.... do it again. podtssoaoa is a good game looks good. still beta af one day may be alpha unlike me. id pay $20 for it but i paid $23 cause im a wild man. if you kiss your homies good night or if your wife sleeps in late and you get saturday mornings to game with your one unemployed friend... but he also sleeps in so you dont play together because when he wakes up your wife makes you go to a garden center again then this is the game for you... or smite 2 smite 2 is also good

As someone who played Path of Exile 1, I was honestly excited for Path of Exile 2. Thankfully, it turned out to be everything I hoped for and more. It keeps the depth and crazy build variety that made PoE1 so addicting, but the combat feels way better since you can "WASD" keys now for better kiting, more satisfying, and actually makes boss fights fun instead of just melting everything in seconds (Im a monk lol). The biggest thing for me is how easy it is to lose track of time. I'll sit down thinking, "Alright, just one more quest," then one quest turns into another, then another, and suddenly I hear birds outside and realize the sun is already up and I have to go to work lol. It's one of those games that completely sucks you in without you even noticing. Whether I'm tweaking my build, hunting for better gear, or getting destroyed by a boss for the fifth time, I'm always having a good time. As a PoE1 player, PoE2 feels familiar enough to make me feel at home, but different enough that it feels like a whole new adventure. It's honestly the kind of game that makes you forget what time it is.
Diablo 4 but better and classes and gameplay are not locked under paywalls

- Downgraded copy of PoE1 with early access as an excuse to not fix bugs, horrible server lag, bad game engine performance.

- Absolutely abysmal loot, everything is just gambling or buying items from botters(who are around 50% of the people online at any time).

- Horrible community, better keep your ingame chats closed. (Or say something there and be instantly hammered with 100 bot messages about RMT services)

- Gameplay ens up being "kill bosses in 1 second or die to oneshots no matter your defences". This ofcourse means everyone just plays 2 different builds and buys the same gear.

- Do yourself a favor, go play literally any other ARPG, even PoE1.

2000+ hours in Path of Exile and I've been grinding Path of Exile 2 since the start of Early Access.

What keeps me coming back is the dark fantasy atmosphere and the sheer brutality of the combat. The character-building system is an incredible sandbox, offering an almost endless number of playstyles through different class combinations, skills, items, and passive tree choices. Few ARPGs give players this much freedom to experiment and create their own builds.

The graphics are stunning, the combat feels impactful, and the world is packed with memorable monsters, unique lore, and fascinating characters. We've come a long way from the early 0.1 versions to 0.5, and seeing the game evolve has been a pleasure.

If you enjoy action RPGs, dark fantasy, deep character customization, endless loot hunting, and challenging gameplay, this is absolutely a game worth playing.

A special mention also goes to the customer support team. In an industry where good support is increasingly rare, Grinding Gear Games continues to set a high standard.

I can't wait to see what 1.0 brings. Highly recommended.

I had a frustating experience with GGG customer support. I requested a refund through Steam after the game went on sale the same day I purchased it. GGG responded by locking my account entirely. When I contacted their customer support, the tone was accusatory, implying I had done something wrong, when I was simply trying to benefit from a sale like any reasonable customer would. 

A company that punishes customers and then lectures them.

I really want to give this game a good review but it constantly makes decisions which I feel are wrong; The endgame is setup with rush mobs in a game which wants you to have multiple button builds, lack of crafting options available like the bench from poe1 force players to buy using the market, run maps constantly looking for a specific base item or buy from players via the trade menu/website. 

The game is full of bot accounts within trade and allowance of these ,and RMTers, ruin the endgame and GGG don't seem to care about them at all. Leagues also hit inflation very quickly and rarely falls until they  are rolled into standard. Vaal league allowed exploiters and day 1 temple builders to make temples which allowed "printing" 100s of divine a day; but stopping new temple builders from achieving the same methods. Removing essences which gave T1 modifiers and nerfing them just to force the longevity of the grind of end game.

The game has an identity crisis with what it wants you to build around, they change up evasion rating and armour to make us tankier but we get one shot by nasty modifiers randomly, mobs having slow modifiers which feel predatory, mana siphoning mobs which you cant run away from, metas being nerfed constantly without any good alternative buffs. The best defence in this game is to one shot a room instantly. Dex's evasion rating and deflection is simply gambling if youll be hit ,or hit for 50%, which is no way reliable even at 60-80% so you are forced to build for a hybrid evasion - energy shield (Which recently got a notable node nerf).

The trade community and the community in general are some of the nicest and most toxic I've experienced in my time playing games, from constantly having to be aware of scammers while trading and buying items to people legit saying slurs towards me. 

The game runs awfully at endgame due to huge amounts of effects and monsters, the servers (For EU) suck ass constantly going between 20ms to 80ms every few seconds for London and if I use any US server apart from Washington I get huge latency spikes, I've had to multiple times delete my shader cache for the game due to sfx issues and crashes.

I have a love - hate relationship with this game and would advise anyone to be cautious about getting into the game at 0.5

To access 1/3 of your build you've got to slog through a dungeon that forces you to play completely different to the game, it actively punishes melee builds as it's gimmic is "don't take damage" via a second health bar called honour. can't reccomend the game as that system is inferior to PoE's Ascendency dungeon.

100% recommend. I'm not a hardcore gamer, but I've played MMOs casually and usually with friends. But then I decided to really learn to play, on my own, without carries, learn to play my class and understand the game mechanics, actually dig into it...for some reason, I decided to do that with PoE2...and while it seems overwhelming, especially when you look at the massive skill tree and the endless possibilities of builds,...the game somehow still sucked me in and didn't let me give up. I pushed through to end game, on my own, finally, and it felt so frickin' rewarding. That ol' skool gaming feeling of....we earned it. Anyhow, it might be a weird thing to write in a review, but the game gave me that feeling of accomplishment, hours of enjoyment and a welcomed addition to my regular lineup of to-go games.

After 6 hours, I'm genuinely impressed.

Not by the game, mind you. I'm impressed that Grinding Gear Games managed to spend a decade learning lessons from Path of Exile 1 and then immediately forget all of them.

Path of Exile 2 feels less like a sequel and more like a corporate amnesia experiment.

Want to move? Input lag.

Want to attack? Hope the skill actually goes where you aimed it.

Want to use a movement skill? Exciting! You'll discover where you're traveling at the same time your character does.

Want to dodge an attack? Better start rolling now. And again. And again. And again. I haven't dodge-rolled this much since I played Sonic the Hedgehog.

The combat isn't difficult because enemies are dangerous. It's difficult because you're fighting the controls, the terrain, the camera, the visual clarity, and occasionally the laws of physics.

Enemy attacks are covered in beautiful visual effects that range from "completely harmless decoration" to "instant death laser." The game expects you to identify which is which while the screen looks like someone dropped a Christmas tree into a particle accelerator.

I've been clipped into terrain. I've watched abilities fail to land where I aimed. I've had movement skills decide that my intended destination was merely a suggestion. I've taken damage from mechanics that felt less avoidable and more like the game politely informing me that my health bar was no longer a democracy.

The most shocking part is that PoE1 already solved so many of these problems. Quality-of-life features, responsiveness, movement, readability—years of iteration and feedback. Instead of building on that foundation, GGG apparently buried it in a shallow grave and started over with a whiteboard that said:

"WHAT IF EVERYTHING FELT WORSE?"

To be fair, they did carry over my MTX from PoE1.

So congratulations, GGG. The cosmetics I purchased years ago work perfectly while the gameplay does not. Gold star for that.

Maybe it'll be great someday. But right now? This isn't the sequel I was waiting for.

It's a dodge-roll simulator with occasional ARPG elements.

Great game gameplay wise but the technical issues are so crazy to me. 

Crashing, frozen screens, having to terminate process constantly, lagging when trying to view atlas map. 

9800x3d and 4070ti, no framerate issues, game looks great.

I really want to like this game and part of me does but I cannot recommend it unless you are unemployed with masochistic tendencies. Or just older lad with holiday and want game that causes you to burst in rage sometimes to also start it up again just moment later.

I love ARPG-genre, I have played poe1 way too much so I knew what this game will be, however I had hoped they made it a bit more friendly for regular player.
This game is far from new player friendly, you will spend at least 2 characters and copious amount of time to either research or simply playing to understand it.

First problem is performance, it is just bad. Game looks really nice but on this type of game where you need quick responses or you are dead, bad performance gives you gray hairs. Also, long loading screens in 2026 regardless of specs and quite a lot of bugs from mild annoyance to simply game breaking is quite iffy.

Second is developers goal, I mean are they trying to create game that one plays for fun or to punish themselves.
Currently it is more of latter and I wonder myself why I want to play this even more.
A lot of bosses and encounters have literally one shotting mechanics and bosses having invulnerable periods. Dodging long time one shots and then dying to lag is not fun design.
Also there is TONS of clutter. Your screen is full either your own effects or encounter effects so try to figure out of all that what kills you.

Third is balance and loot it is either you have very good build or utter trash. Almost all skills fall to latter category which is sad, unless you spend a lot time farming on starter to get niche items to enable build. (And even then it is on on few)
You either pick build you can breeze without gear, since it won't drop, or spend a lots of time farming.

Fourth is related to second but simply not listening players. Everyone hates trials and say their issues with current end game, yet no feedback about this.
Sprinting, I have not heard anyone actually liking this addition to game. Just make maps smaller instead of this stupid mechanic.

I REALLY want to recommend and love this game but as working adult, I cannot recommend it even though pausing game is massive qol but time investment to reach actual endgame is still huge. Unless you are one of those who already have spent hundred to thousands of hours and know game ins and outs.

Somewhere around level 40 this game started to feel immensely unsatisfying. Maybe I'm just terrible at seeing through the numbers but I genuinely couldn't figure out how to make my build not suck. I fully respec'd like twice trying to resolve survivability/DPS issues and just couldn't get my character to a place that felt satisfying. I even tried "crafting" with the dizzying amount of currency items I had to try and improve my build with no luck. Maybe if you can see through the numbers or are willing to stick to someone else's build guide there's some fun here. But this game is about the end game and if I'm not having fun getting there, what's the likelihood it'll be better when I arrive?

I like it, but know before you buy that it wasn’t meant to replace PoE and doesn’t play the same - it’s slower and weightier, which some may dislike. The graphics are beautiful and the story is repetitive. Performance is good aswell.
Good game, it keeps you on your toes. Gorgeous graphics and very good music. I recommend it if you like Diablo-style games.

As much as I want to love the game, and I do enjoy playing it with my brother, the developers don't seem to understand how to make a game fun, I did enjoy my time, and would play next season just for the boys, but this game is seriously struggling, the dev's are borderline retarded and retain only the barest semblance of how to create an enjoyable experience.
I don't really get it... you just grind, to grind more, and pay for inventory tab, to grind more...

Lol, still no swords. At this point it feels like spite towards the general demographic of fantasy enjoyers. 

A fantasy ARPG launching without the defining fantasy weapon is already bizarre. Still not having them after this long feels less like an omission and more like a statement.

Swords are not some obscure niche request. They are the default weapon of the genre, arguably its most iconic symbol.

Maybe GGG is being contrarian. Maybe they just somehow missed the pulse of the genre they’re making a game in. I don’t really care which. Both answers are embarrassing.

I am posting this review during early access, during the Fate of the Vaal league.

Path of Exile 2 is a frustrating game to think about. In a bubble, it's quite good, especially for game that isn't fully complete. The story is good, the game looks beautiful, builds are fun and varied, and the skill system is genuinely inspired. Unfortunately, this isn't a game that exists in a bubble. It has a lot of competition between Diablo 4, Diablo 2 Resurrected, Lost Ark, and Path of Exile 1. I haven't played Lost Ark, personally, but I can say that this game compares poorly against Diablo and Path of Exile 1.

I should also note that I have put about 1,000 hours into Path of Exile 1, and have been playing since that game's Bestiary League (2018).

STORY
=================

The game's story picks up a few years after Path of Exile 1's story ends. I genuinely really liked the story in this game. It was fun getting to see some returning characters from the first game, as well as seeing new faces and going to new places that were only mentioned in the first in either flavor text or off-handedly in dialog. I think PoE 1's twists with Sin/Innocence still shines brighter than any one plot point here, but PoE2's story is really solid.

Gameplay
===================

This is where I have most of my problems, so let's start with some positives.

I really like the new skill gem system. I think it's a massive improvement over the socket/link system from PoE1, and allowing the user to just engrave whatever skills they want into a blank skill gem is a wonderful idea.

I also really like that the passive tree now allows you to allocate whatever stat you want into the small nodes instead of giving you a set Int/Strength/Dex. This will help avoid situations where you have way more of one stat than you really need, but are scrounging around for some extra Dex to be able to use the gear/skills you want. This combined with my previous point makes building new characters much easier than before.

Finally, I am so glad we finally have async trading. This exists in PoE1 now too, but it really is such a massive quality of life increase that I wanted to mention it here. Relying on trading sites and hoping people responded to pings added HOURS to each build, just hoping that you can get the gear you want. Now it's not a problem because you can just search for items people are selling in-game, go to their hideout, and buy straight from their vendor. Genuinely gamechanging stuff.

I really love the new ascendancies. I played the Druid for my playthrough, and had a lot of fun. So much so that it made me wish that the PoE2 ascendancies were available in PoE1 so I could play them even more.

Unfortunately, that's sort of it for the positives.

The game is just too damn slow. In my opinion, the thing that set PoE1 apart from games like Diablo was speed. By act 4 or so, you should be mowing through mobs and zipping across the map. It gave the game a real feeling of building power and having a real tactile experience of becoming stronger. By endgame, you can walk into a room and instantly explode 300 enemies with a single attack. Maybe even just your aura is enough to explode enemies, and you end up pressing 0 buttons. None of this is present in PoE2. You move slow, there are no movement abilities in the game, there are no leveling uniques to make building second/third/etc characters easier, enemies have more health, and bosses both have more health and have more invuln stages. The whole game feels like you're playing PoE 1 in slow motion. Also, with the new design philosophy for skills, it's basically impossible to make a decent build that doesn't require at least 3-4 button presses per pack of mobs to kill. Every part of combat feels sluggish and uneven. 

I should note that gameplay in Diablo 4 is similarly slow, especially at higher difficulties. Builds require multiple buttons, and bosses have a lot of health. But Diablo 4 still makes you feel powerful. You can feel your character getting stronger as you level. I didn't feel powerful at the end of PoE2's story, I just felt tired.

I cannot believe that we are still having to "feel the weight" of all of the crafting materials we pick up by clicking every single one. Mercifully, GGG decided a few years ago to allow currency to stack so you can pick up a 5x Chromatic stack instead of having to pick up 5 separate Chromatic Orbs, but it really sucks that PoE2 didn't adjust this further by just allowing us to auto-pick up crafting items. If they're worried about flooding inventory space, maybe just allow players to toggle what they want to pick up automatically, and what to leave behind. Or, look at their competitors. Diablo 4 just auto picks up all of your crafting items and puts them in a separate inventory for you. Why are we still doing it this way?

I hate the dodge roll mechanic. It feels so slow and ineffective compared to any of the movement skills from PoE1. The sprint similarly feels bad. It isn't as fast as I feel like it should be, and getting attacked while sprinting trips you so you just lay there and get attacked for free. It makes me not want to sprint at all, which is a problem because the maps in PoE2 are massive and have almost no roadmarking, so your first few times through an area, you'll probably cover the entire map before you find where you need to go. And you're doing it all at a snail's pace. It's not fun, I'm sorry. PoE1 had some big maps too, but you could Flame Dash over gaps to create your own short cuts, or just generally move faster in that game. I mentioned earlier that Diablo 4 is a similarly slow game to PoE2, but at least Diablo gives you a horse eventually.

Why is there no life on the passive tree? All it does is make life on gear even more valuable, and gear without life rolls basically worthless. Mechanics like ward or the upcoming Runes giving you a free revive feel glued together to excuse the lack of general life gained from traversing the tree.

Finally, Act 3 is just miserable. It's really long, really slow, has a lot of massive maps to traverse, and almost made me quit the game. The whole time I was in act 3, I kept wishing I was playing PoE1 instead. I think the whole thing needs a major rework.

Graphics
==================

I think the game looks beautiful, especially some of the zones in Act 2. The PoE art style/aesthetic has aged really well, and I think they did a great job here.

Bugs
==================

During my playthrough of just the acts, my game crashed about 5 times. Mostly in acts 2 and 3 for some reason. PoE1 was never a very well-optimized game, but it is disappointing to see that PoE2 seems to have the same problems. It would also occasionally drop frames or hang entirely. This seemed to mostly happen when a lot of effects were going off, but sometimes it'd just happen when no combat was occurring at all.

I noticed multiple instances of textures loading incorrectly, or taking a long time to load. This isn't gamebreaking, but it did happen often enough that I wanted to mention it.

Conclusion
===================

PoE2 feels like a game having an identity crisis to me. It is pulling a lot from PoE1 and contemporary games like Diablo, but doesn't seem to really know how to put it all together yet. As-is, this feels like a game targeted entirely at PoE1 diehards who are sick and tired of killing Kitava for the millionth time and can do Maven with their eyes closed. But to anyone else, I think you'd be better off just playing PoE1, Diablo or Lost Ark.

I am still interested in where this game will go. I started playing PoE1 in 2018, and the PoE1 of today is so different from that version that it's genuinely impressive it took them this long to make a sequel. That game has been ship of theseus'd so many times I'm not sure if any part of the original remains. So I am curious to see where this game will be in a few years.
The bugs make it a frustrating mess, there is something good in there but its just not ready.

A bloated-HP-bar-apalooza. A skindeep Soulslike-aspirant that shoddily copied Diablo IV's homework, after Diablo IV shoddily copied PoE1's homework.  Defining difficulty by "you have a dodge button, so for every 15 seconds of combat, spam it for 10 of those seconds, otherwise it's your fault, you suck, or you aren't high enough level to have the right skills.  By the way, you suck."  Unless you just twink your character by building it according to various meta builds online, in which case, since someone else played the game and planned the build for you, just read a summary of the story somewhere, and play something with fun as the core focus.

For anyone who remembers Dungeon Defenders, remember when that shmuck said for Dungeon Defenders 2, "Copy everything from League of Legends.  If Riot does it, we do it,"?  This game is that, for a supersaturated subgenre that I honestly wish never existed.  About the only thing it doesn't do is drop all your gold on the floor on death.

I continue to try to play this every now and then to wash the utter disappointment out of my mouth, thinking after months of patches and feedback that someone at GGG will listen to something other than cash rolling in from cosmetics, and player numbers soaring because content creators perfected making the game look palatable by already having their builds set up.  It is an exercise in futility.

I liked PoE1's story.  I wanted to see where it headed.  But just like Diablo 4 pissed all over Diablo 3's hanging threads, PoE2 assumes you're an unga-bunga content creator, and thus mashing through dialogue.  And given the production team's prohibitive "not everyone's meant to get through this," mentality, I'll just have to settle for watching lore videos if I don't, in fact, git gud.

I have other options.  So do you.  Play those instead.
"Our biggest expansion" for an early access game. Read that out loud to yourself.

Fun game at first. But the lack of a solo self found mode that is balanced for SSF detracts significantly. You're forced to engage with trading if you want to play the end game or avoid massive dry spells of loot.
As the games got updated ive had more issues. sometimes its s 2 mins to load in sometimes its 10 seconds. Stuttering. game freezing in the settings, audio delays etc. Used to work fine.

Absolutely frustrating experience when it comes to the controller.
One of the most unintuitive solutions I ever saw.
Please fire the person responsible for this crap.

Apart from the game crashing every 20-40 minutes (Ryzen5 5600x 6700xt videocard). The game is extremely unforgiving towards people who refuse to do cafeine or cocaine, you need perfect reflexes or you get oneshotted or two-tabbed, or crowd controlled into oblivion, regardless of the amount of defense you have and dodgerolling is sometimes hard to predict which direction you will go. The itemisation is severely anti-fun. because it is impossible to change some things without breaking attributes on either your gear, your gems, or any very small interactions with the result of having to grind 10 hours to respec, which then requires new respecs, new gear, new attributes etc. just to make some tiny changes. The market is already destroyed by scalpers to the point that even the most basic things are way to expensive i saw basic support gems go for 5 exalteds or 1 divine, which takes ages to farm. The game is also very immoral, because it makes everything hidden or occult, which means: it doesn't give you enough information to really understand it, so you are working with guides, youtube, learning every single word in the game like for instance leeching resistance or honour resistance. You name it, it is stupidly annoying and after a tiny mistake you find out that your minion actually is not a companion, so you had skillpoints invested, which then needs to be respecked... Going through the campaign is mostly terrible for getting stuck all the time, especially if you have any minions with you. Another thing apart from the second act getting you stucked all the time is the terrible 3rd act, you will get hit by crowd control garbage inside of the swamp, like the makers of the game are literally ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ over you, while you are taking your cross uphill with a crown of thorns, that is how bad it is. The skillgems make things even worse. In any other ARPG you can switch or respec skills, but in POE you need certain extremely rare materials to add up to 5 support sockets to skillgems, and then you need to grind 1 divine orb to get an essential support gem, which you couldn't make or find and players on the market want to destroy your fun, so they make everything filthy expensive, you can not make this nasty garbage up!  

I uninstalled the game after failing the trials of ascendency to often. I will never finish it, because i take anti allergy medications, that increase reaction time to about 0.5 seconds or longer i get killed before seeing the ability that did it.

The only positives are:
1. The game is very beautiful.
2. The game is an awful lot of fun to watch great streamers play hardcore SSF in competition, it is the best progamer competition of any ARPG. But maybe i just like to watch it more than playing it.
3. You get about 40 hours of campaign for very little money, especially compared to diablo4 for instance, which was 3 times more expensive so far.
4. GGG did create a game that forces you to play hundreds of hours to even understand the tiniest of details, it is not for most gamers, but GGG seems to be doing something good for extreme hardcore cucks, who like severely punishing gameplay (or like to hatewatch it).

Not as fun as I expected.  Honestly too complex for its own good.   So many dumb quality of life decisions, which I understand because it's a game designed to be free but damn its annoying.  Why are you keeping identifying scrolls in this day and age, other than to add a step to a masked shallow loot system.  Why is the inventory so small that with 3 armor pieces it's full?  Developers have no respect of players time?!  Stupid design decisions... I guess I've just grown tired of made up ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ systems that add "depth" to the game but in reality they're just a waste of time and there to get you to spend money.  Many things like this make the game not fun for me so therefore I say skip it.

[h2]poe2 is a beautiful game.[/h2]

as a fan of the original grim Diablo II in the past, and not a fan of modern ARPGs, I was hesitating whether to buy it. I did not like the first PoE either, for some reason. Guess it was its old-ass point'n'click movement instead of WASD.
When I finally decided to buy it, I went with a more expensive edition to get that extra feeling of being rewarded with something right from the beginning.

[b][u]This game is magnificent. [/u][/b]Locations, items, visual effects - everything is made so well that the game could easily be played in first-person and you would not think it was never planned for that.
[b][u]Gameplay is immaculate.[/u][/b] It is meditative, catchy, and scratches your ADHD just right. You do not have that “grind to grind more” feeling here, because each action feels like a step towards beating the campaign or the endgame storyline.
The most beautiful part is that it is online and [b]your progress has some value[/b]. You cannot just cheat your way up, or throw money at devs for pay-to-win items. You have to earn them, or grind currencies to exchange with other players for the item you want.
[b][u]Endgame is BIG. [/u][/b]And when I say BIG, I mean you can spend thousands of hours on the open map and still not see everything the game has to offer. You may want to live inside this game, though you may also get tired of the repetitive cycle much faster. A cycle, that really does get more repetitive than just meditative after some time. It's good thing different characters make the game feel really different and fresh, as well as tons of different modifiers and activities on the global map.

The only obvious flaws are questionable optimization (I'm saying skill spamming kills performance) and weird server connection, where server lag on ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Frankfurt for 0.5 second could cost you your expensive items. Aside of that - this game is the best that had happened to arpg and diablo-like games.
[h3]I'm not sure I'll spend thousands or even hundreds of hours here, but I surely will play this after the 1.0 release and will definitely max-out a few characters. And for now I just enjoy the endgame[/h3]

I like PoE 2.

Perhaps I'm a bit overwhelmed with it and need to take a break from the game and come back at release.

The developers have done a lot of work during Early Access, especially in the endgame.

A huge amount of quality of life has been added, many controversial issues have been reworked, character classes and skills have been constantly refined, and I'm very happy with that.

We now have things that players have been dreaming about since PoE 1, namely: an in-game marketplace, offline trading with other players, and the ability to import builds directly into the game. For that alone, I'm ready to support the developers.

For me, the main drawback is probably the size of the locations, especially in the first three acts.

I don't understand why the developers don't want to reduce the size of the locations in the first three acts, especially in Act 3. They added all sorts of hints on where to go, but I'm used to clearing locations to the limit, and that takes a lot of time.
Spend some money on POE 1, glad to see that getting ported to POE 2, more soulslike/action based than the first game tho
Great gameplay, awesome character buildings, dungeons and boss fights... and ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ server that constantly ruins my enjoyment of the game.

I really want to like the game, but GGG is making it very hard with their weird and disrespectful decisions. Since they introduced the ability to travel between checkpoints, maps get pseudo-reset when you challenge the map boss (don't even have to actually engage it in combat yet, stepping into the boss arena at all counts) and respawn after dying. That means, ALL content but the map boss gets scrubbed off the map. No mobs, no breach, delirium, ritual, etc. If you died during delirium, after stepping into the boss arena, that delirium is completely gone without any rewards or any signs it even existed. 

We have now had multiple seasons with this and I considered it a bug, but considering that GGG is not doing anything about it, I suppose it is a feature and it is easily the worst feature of the endgame by far. Getting knocked down from sprinting by any hit - okay, I just don't sprint outside of town, I guess. Having only a single life on a map - okay, I play extra careful and do the most rewarding content first. But that boss pseudo-reset thing is just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. It forces you to do the boss as the very last thing, because if you do something like the new breach stronghold maps, it doesn't even count the map as completed. It removes everything but the boss and still expects you to destroy the breach hives - the same breach hives that no longer exist on the empty map you have after dying to the boss once. Difficulty mechanics have to serve a purpose to not feel like arbitrary ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that is simply there to waste your time. Deadly traps force you to pay more attention to your surroundings and be more wary in general. Tough bosses force you to learn their patterns, making them more interactive. The sprint knockdown just makes sprint barely usable on any area that isn't the town, since even a single crab in a corner you forgot could knock you down and waste your time with the longest knockdown animation they could come up with. The boss map reset just takes away your agency when it comes to when you do the boss. You are forced to do it last. If you wanted to do the boss first, to make sure you don't fail the map and make the most out of the 3 lives you got on it - well you just lose all content on the map for dying once against him. Even if it means losing 2 lives on other content first and then potentially losing your last on the boss, you are forced to do it that way, because doing the boss first is way too risky no matter how many lives you have, due to it nuking the entire map if you die once. Extremely frustrating and stupid mechanic, that encourages nothing and only punishes player choice. And again, it has been a well-established, well-known thing across multiple seasons now, so either GGG is incapable of fixing it or leaving it like this intentionally.

I'm a gamer girl in her gamer world w poe2. So addicting. 100/10. 

But for the developers a note: in ascension 2 its buggy w two players on the platform and we had to reload bc it wouldn't let the platform go back down for the other person. Also, in big moments, like boss fights, there seems to be server graphic issues and disconnects that lead to the cog wheels loading you back in.

So I feel like the game has had enough time to cook to actually give a review after over 400 hours. I think that as of 0.5 Path of Exile 2 finally feels like the game it needed to be on the surface. I played a character per league every league since early access trying to play at least 1 different class arch type every league to get a "start to finish" idea every time, and I can say this is the first league where I felt like this game can finally be a successor to Path 1. While Path 1 will always have its ardent dedicated fan base because it is a more traditional ARPG. I feel like the balancing of this game has finally hit a point where people running the non meta class builds have a chance to experience end game, and not just experience it, but experience a version of endgame that feels like a whole second game after the campaign. The amount of story writing and interweaving of the mechanics and storylines into the campaign and the mapping concepts was done very well and naturally in universe.

As for the campaign I do appreciate the work that has gone into streamlining it. i think the shift of the Azak bog to the waterways needs to be explained a bit better in game, but I think its movement was a good choice. I would like, however, more changes like the water ways level where when you do it once you don't have to do it again. 

I think that the combat balancing has come a long way since the start. the first few leagues for combat balancing was brutal with how I felt slow and the enemies all felt like they were swarming which got even worse in mapping. I am happy that not only is this a better experience but it is also nice to see that luck isn't as much of a determining factor for mapping progression and I can finally enjoy mid to end game mapping content outside of a meta build thats not runnings a TON of item discovery.

Frankly I'm excited for 1.0 I think that in these past leages there have been good leagues. There have been ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ leagues (Looking at you azmiri spirits) but I think the game ultimately is in a place where I can confidently say that if you want an ARPG that mixes souls action combat and ARPG dungeoncrawling and loot pinata content POE 2 is a good game to play.
